abstract: Despite the rapid proliferation of carbon dots (CDs) in materials science, the fundamental mechanisms governing their formation remain poorly understood. This study elucidates the synthesis of CDs via a one-step hydrothermal treatment of 4-aminoantipyrine. By systematically investigating the evolution of the material across varying reaction intervals, we provide a detailed schematic of the polymerization and carbonization pathways. This graphical representation offers critical insights into the sequential stages of CD development, clarifying the chemical transitions essential to their formation.
